message:
Render war story detail overlay via Portal to escape stacking contexts

The war stories tactical map detail overlay was rendered as an
absolutely-positioned child inside the map container, which itself
lives inside FrankenContainer elements with CSS transforms and filters.
These properties create new stacking contexts, trapping the overlay's
z-index within the parent subtree — meaning it could be clipped by
or painted behind sibling sections.

Fix: Add a Portal helper (createPortal to document.body) matching
the pattern already established in BottomSheet, screenshot gallery
lightbox, and beads detail modal. The overlay now renders as a direct
child of <body>, fully escaping ancestor stacking contexts.

Additional changes:
- Change overlay from absolute to fixed positioning (inset-0) so it
  covers the full viewport rather than just the map container bounds.
- Bump z-index from z-20 to z-[999] for consistency with other
  Portal-based overlays across the site.
- Separate the backdrop (opacity fade) from the content card (scale +
  translate) into distinct motion.div elements so the backdrop fades
  smoothly while the card slides up independently.
- Widen detail card from max-w-2xl to max-w-3xl to give war story
  content more breathing room.
- Enhance the close button with shadow glow, active:scale-95 press
  feedback, more padding, and rename label to CLOSE_BATTLE_REPORT
  for thematic consistency with the tactical map aesthetic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
